citations.description: test_inbound_write_stamps_and_signs_as_identity asserts author_email, author_id, author_sig present but never calls rebar verify-authorship or equivalent
Implementation correctly calls attribution_fields and _apply_authorship inline

findings.criterion: _write_event_file stamps author_email/author_id (via _seam.attribution_fields) and signs author_sig (via _seam._apply_authorship) inline before writing; a reconciler-authored event written with the bot identity + a signing key verifies as `verified` under `rebar verify-authorship` (proven by a unit test that configures a bot identity + signing key, drives `_write_event_file`, and asserts the written event carries `author_id`/`author_email`/`author_sig` and verifies).
findings.detail: The code changes in `_write_event_file` correctly stamp attribution and sign events inline. The unit test `test_inbound_write_stamps_and_signs_as_identity` configures a bot identity + signing key, drives `_write_event_file`, and asserts `author_email`, `author_id`, and `author_sig` are present. However, the criterion explicitly requires the test to assert the event 'verifies as `verified` under `rebar verify-authorship`'. No call to `verify_authorship`, `verify_identity`, or equivalent verification command is made anywhere in the test file — the test only checks that `author_sig` is a non-empty value, not that the signature actually verifies.

citations.description: test_inbound_write_additive_no_identity checks the write succeeds and event_type is correct, but never invokes the reducer or rebar show to assert round-trip cleanly
Implementation correctly writes an uncommitted file (no write_and_push), preserving batch-commit model

findings.criterion: Additive-fields regression test: a unit test constructs an event WITHOUT the new author fields (older-format) and asserts the reducer / `rebar show` still round-trips it cleanly (unknown/absent keys ignored, no format break). The batch-commit orchestration is unchanged (no eager commit/push added to the inbound path).
findings.detail: The test `test_inbound_write_additive_no_identity` verifies that `_write_event_file` writes an event without the new author fields when no identity resolves, confirming the write itself succeeds. However, the criterion requires asserting that the reducer / `rebar show` still round-trips such an event cleanly. No test calls `reduce_ticket`, `rebar show`, or any reducer/display path on an older-format event (one constructed without `author_email`/`author_id`/`author_sig`) to assert no format break. The batch-commit invariant is preserved in the code (no write_and_push added), but there is also no dedicated test asserting this invariant.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
